title: AD Taylor-tower P11: tower-based user numerics -- Taylor-series ODE integration, Householder-family root refinement, and local functional inversion via series reversion
goal: Implement docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md section 13 (Phase P11). Expose the univariate Taylor tower (`taylor`/`derivative-n`, Phases P1/P2/P6) as general-purpose numerical methods: a recursive/automatic Taylor-series ODE integrator for y'=f(t,y), a Householder-family (Newton/Halley/order-k) root refiner, and a local functional inverse via Lagrange-inversion series reversion, all reduced to ONE primitive (`taylor`) with zero codegen changes -- pure new-file Scheme orchestration, following core.ad.guw's (P4) precedent.
acceptance: taylor-ode-solve: y'=y, y(0)=1 -> y(1)=e converges to < 1e-10 at order 8-14 with a handful of fixed steps; error-vs-order table (fixed step budget) printed, showing geometric/spectral convergence down to the double-precision floor (~4.4e-16) by order 12-14

taylor-ode-solve: y'=-y^2, y(0)=1 -> y(t)=1/(1+t) matches the closed form to < 1e-10 at several t (0.25, 1, 2); has a larger error constant than the linear case (nonlinear derivative growth) but converges with the same qualitative order/step shape

taylor-root: x^2-2 from x0=1 -- order=1 (Newton, quadratic), order=2 (Halley, cubic) and order=4 all converge to sqrt(2) to <1e-14; an independent hand-written classical Halley-formula cross-check (different code path) run 4 times from the same x0 shows textbook cubic convergence (iter errors 1.4e-2, 3.6e-7, 2.2e-16, 0); a transcendental root cos(x)=x (Dottie number) also converges
taylor-inverse-series: exp around x0=0.0 -> log(1+y) coefficients 0, 1, -1/2, 1/3, -1/4, ... to order 8, verified against the closed form and via a round-trip exp(h(w)) = 1+w numeric cross-check

taylor-inverse-series EXACT case (P6): f(x)=x+x^2 at an exact integer x0=0 -- reversion is the alternating-sign Catalan-number series (hand-derived independently via undetermined coefficients: 0,1,-1,2,-5,14,-42,132,-429), verified bit-exact with `exact?` + `=`; a second exact case at a non-integer exact RATIONAL x0=1/2 -- reversion is the binomial series for sqrt(1+w)-1 (1/2,-1/8,1/16,-5/128,7/256,-21/1024), also bit-exact
taylor-eval (shared Horner evaluator) verified standalone and via self-consistency with `taylor`'s own coefficient lists

Zero codegen/runtime changes: pure new-file Scheme module in lib/core/ad/, self-contained (own list/power-series helpers, no stdlib map/for-each/list-ref/keyword-arg dependency -- two pre-existing, previously undocumented compiler limitations were hit and worked around, not fixed: see ESH-0220 and ESH-0221)

Regressions (both -r and AOT): taylor_tower_test.esk 52/52, taylor_tower_mono_test.esk 441/441, exact_taylor_test.esk 68/68, guw_multivariate_test.esk 35/35, run_ad_oracle.sh 56/56, run_sicp_smoke.sh 88/88
New test tests/ad/taylor_numerics_test.esk (41 checks) PASS under BOTH -r and AOT and wired into CTest (taylor_numerics_runtime_smoke)
campaign_payoff: The Taylor tower becomes a general-purpose numerical kernel, not just an internal AD representation: users get an arbitrary-order ODE integrator, superlinear/cubic+ root finding, and exact local series inversion (with genuine bit-exact rational output for polynomial/rational functions at exact points, a capability no double-based system offers) for free, all from the one univariate `taylor` primitive already shipped in P1/P2/P6.

delivered.module: lib/core/ad/taylor_numerics.esk (new core AD module, require core.ad.taylor_numerics). Self-contained (own tn-length/tn-list-ref/tn-list-set/tn-reverse/tn-zeros/tn-ps-mul helpers), no stdlib map/for-each/list-ref dependency, following lib/core/ad/tape.esk and lib/core/ad/guw.esk's core-module discipline. Provides taylor-eval, taylor-ode-solve, taylor-root, taylor-inverse-series.
delivered.shared_primitive: taylor-eval(coeffs, dt): Horner evaluation of a `taylor`-style coefficient list c[0..k] at an offset dt. Used internally by all three higher-level operators and exported for direct use.
delivered.ode: taylor-ode-solve(f, y0, t0, t1, k, n): classic recursive/automatic Taylor-series method. Builds the order-k Taylor coefficients b[0..k] of the LOCAL solution y(t+h) one order at a time: at step i, having the CORRECT b[0..i] already (a genuine tower property, not an approximation, since formal power-series composition is lower-triangular), a fresh order-i `taylor` call on g(h)=f(t+h, Y_i(h)) (Y_i the partial polynomial built so far) recovers the exact i-th Taylor coefficient of y'(t+h), giving b[i+1]=c[i]/(i+1). Advances n fixed steps from t0 to t1 via taylor-eval.
delivered.root: taylor-root(f, x0, k): order-k Householder refinement via series reversion. `(taylor f x k)` gives c[0..k] at the current iterate; dropping c[0] gives g(h)=f(x+h)-f(x) (zero constant term, revertible). The correction is the reversion series h(w) evaluated at w=-f(x). k=1 collapses exactly to Newton's step, k=2 to Halley's method (verified algebraically and empirically against an independent hand-written classical-formula implementation).
delivered.inversion: taylor-inverse-series(f, x0, k): same reversion, reported directly as f^-1(f(x0)+w) = x0 + h(w), i.e. the coefficient list (x0 . reversion of (taylor f x0 k) with c[0] zeroed).
delivered.reversion_algorithm: tn-series-reversion: Lagrange inversion by undetermined coefficients over truncated power series (tn-ps-mul: Cauchy product truncated to degree k). a[1]=1/c[1]; for n=2..k, a[n] is solved in closed form from a[1..n-1] alone (a[n] itself acts as a zero placeholder while summing, since a degree-n coefficient of a(w)^i for i>=2 provably cannot depend on the still-unknown a[n]). Pure +,-,*,/ arithmetic, so it preserves EXACT (P6) coefficients whenever f's own tower coefficients come back exact.
delivered.argument_style: All arguments are plain positional (NOT the design doc's #:order/#:steps keyword-arg sketch) -- keyword-arg formals are unusable inside any `(require ...)`-loaded module (ESH-0220, newly discovered and filed, not fixed as part of this phase).
delivered.bugs_worked_around_not_fixed: A second, more severe pre-existing bug was discovered and worked around (not fixed): a `taylor` call whose lambda argument closes over a self-tail-recursive (TCO'd/PHI-loop) function's OWN loop-carried parameter reads a stale/corrupted value (silently wrong results, or a segfault when the captured value is a list) once the function actually recurses. Filed as ESH-0221. Worked around in tn-ode-coeffs-loop by rebinding the loop-carried f/t parameters into fresh non-loop-carried locals immediately before constructing the closure passed to `taylor` -- verified to produce bit-correct results with a minimal, independently reproduced repro.
delivered.tests: tests/ad/taylor_numerics_test.esk (41 checks): taylor-eval sanity + self-consistency with `taylor`; ODE y'=y (error-vs-order table order=2..14, steps=4) and y'=-y^2 (3 t-values); root x^2-2 at order 1/2/4 plus an independent 4-iteration hand-written Halley cross-check plus cos(x)=x; inverse-series for exp->log(1+y) (order 8, plus a numeric round-trip check) and two EXACT cases (x0=0 Catalan series to order 8, x0=1/2 sqrt(1+w)-1 binomial series to order 6). Registered in CMakeLists.txt as taylor_numerics_runtime_smoke (JIT -r, PASS/FAIL regex on the 'N passed, 0 failed' summary line).

remainder: Adaptive step-size control for taylor-ode-solve is not implemented (fixed n-step subdivision only); the error-vs-order table demonstrates the order/step trade-off well enough for the gate, but a true adaptive integrator (e.g. step-doubling with an order/step-size controller) is future work.

analytic-continue (design doc's fourth API sketch item, crossing-singularity-aware repeated local expansion) was not implemented -- out of the task's explicit scope (ODE/root/inversion/taylor-eval only); it would naturally reuse taylor-eval and the same step-advancing pattern as taylor-ode-solve plus a radius-of-convergence guard, and is a reasonable follow-up once P8 Taylor models (interval remainders) land to make the guard rigorous rather than heuristic.

ESH-0220 (keyword-arg formals unusable inside any required module) and ESH-0221 (taylor-closure-over-TCO'd-loop-parameter corruption) are filed as separate, NOT fixed, compiler bugs; this phase deliberately worked around both rather than attempting a codegen fix, matching the scope of a numerics-API phase rather than a compiler-hardening phase.

taylor-root's convergence loop uses a fixed absolute tolerance (1e-15) and iteration cap (60), not a user-configurable tolerance/iteration-count parameter -- sufficient for every gated case (all converge in well under 10 iterations) but a documented simplification.

verifications.evidence: tests/ad/taylor_numerics_test.esk 41/41 PASS under BOTH -r (JIT) and AOT (exit 0). ODE error-vs-order (y'=y, steps=4): order=2:2.34e-2, 4:7.19e-5, 6:1.06e-7, 8:9.13e-11, 10:5.15e-14, 12:4.44e-16, 14:4.44e-16 (double-precision floor). y'=-y^2 matched 1/(1+t) to <1e-10 at t=0.25 (order10/steps6), t=1 (order12/steps8), t=2 (order14/steps10) -- a larger error constant than the linear case, confirmed by a full order x step empirical sweep, not a bug. Root: x^2-2 order1/2/4 all <1e-14 vs sqrt(2); independent 4-iter hand Halley cross-check errors 1.41e-2, 3.64e-7, 2.22e-16, 0 (textbook cubic doubling of correct digits); cos(x)=x (Dottie 0.7390851332151607) converged at order 2 and 4. Inversion: exp->log(1+y) coefficients matched analytic 1,-1/2,1/3,...,-1/8 to order-appropriate tolerance (tighter orders looser, due to reversion's O(k^3) floating accumulation) plus a numeric round-trip check; EXACT x0=0 case matched the hand-derived Catalan series 0,1,-1,2,-5,14,-42,132,-429 bit-exact (exact? + = for all 9 coefficients); EXACT x0=1/2 case matched the hand-derived sqrt(1+w)-1 binomial series 1/2,1/2,-1/8,1/16,-5/128,7/256,-21/1024 bit-exact. Regressions: taylor_tower_test.esk 52/52, taylor_tower_mono_test.esk 441/441, exact_taylor_test.esk 68/68, guw_multivariate_test.esk 35/35 (all -r AND AOT); run_ad_oracle.sh 56/56; run_sicp_smoke.sh 88/88. New CTest taylor_numerics_runtime_smoke PASS (1.25s), alongside guw_multivariate_runtime_smoke PASS (1.67s).
